children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: The Commissioner shall make grants under this section to States on a formula basis for the purpose of improving the coordination of services provided to children, youth, and families.

the independent State body is committed to interagency planning that results in statewide policies promoting systematic collaboration among agencies on behalf of young individuals as demonstrated by joint planning, joint financing, joint service delivery, common intake and assessment, and other arrangements that reduce barriers to services and promote more effective local service delivery systems for young individuals;

children.label: A
children.text: age, sex, race, and ethnicity;

children.label: B
children.text: the residences of such individuals;

children.label: C
children.text: the incidence of homelessness among such individuals;

children.label: D
children.text: the composition of families of such individuals;

children.label: E
children.text: the economic situations of such individuals;

children.label: F
children.text: the incidence of poverty among such individuals;

children.label: G
children.text: experiences in the care of such individuals away from home;

children.label: H
children.text: the health of such individuals;

children.label: I
children.text: violence in the homes or communities of such individuals;

children.label: J
children.text: the nature of the attachment of such individuals to school and work;

children.label: K
children.text: dropout rates of such individuals from school; and

children.label: L
children.text: the character of the communities in which such individuals reside;

children.label: A
children.text: each area have an equal opportunity to apply for or receive funds under this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: the public be given an opportunity to express views concerning the development and administration of such plan;

the independent State body will provide an inventory of existing public and private services for children, youth and their families and will evaluate the need for supportive services within the State to address the purposes of this chapter and determine the extent to which existing public and private programs meet such need;
the independent State body will make such reports, in such form, and containing such information, as the Commissioner may require;

such fiscal control and fund accounting procedures will be adopted as may be necessary to ensure proper disbursement of, and accounting for, Federal funds paid under this part to the chief executive officer of the State, including any such funds paid to the recipients of a grant or contract;

the independent State body will conduct periodic evaluations of activities and projects carried out pursuant to this section and and will report the results and recommendations to the chief executive officer of the State and the State legislature;section 12338 of this title

the chief executive officer of the State will provide technical assistance or in-service training opportunities for personnel responsible for carrying out the purposes of this section and ; andsection 12338 of this title
the chief executive officer of each State will provide for the implementation of the requirements of , relating to supportive services.section 12338 of this title

children.heading: Use of grants to States
children.label: c Use of grants to States

children.text: the costs of the preparation of such plan and the provision of technical assistance to local areas;
the costs of the evaluation of activities carried out under such plan;
the costs of the collection of data and the carrying out of analyses related to the need for supportive services within the State;
the costs of the dissemination of information obtained under paragraph (3); and
the costs of the provision of short-term training to personnel of public or nonprofit private agencies and organizations engaged in the operation of programs authorized by this part.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant1So in original. No subsec. (d) has been enacted.1
children.label: e Supplement not supplant1So in original. No subsec. (d) has been enacted.1
children.text: Amounts received by a State under this section and shall be used only to supplement, not to supplant, the amount of Federal, State, and local funds expended for the purposes for which grants are made under this section and . In no event shall such expenditures be used to satisfy the matching requirements of any other Federal program.section 12338 of this titlesection 12338 of this title

children.heading: Relationship to family resource and support program grants
children.label: f Relationship to family resource and support program grants
children.text: If a State intends to apply for a grant under section 12339 of this title to be used for the same calendar year as the grant under this section, such State shall include in the application for a grant under this section a description of plans for family resource and support programs and for the coordination of the use of all funds received under this part.2See References in Text note below.2
